title: Spanikopita
ingredients: 1 pound phyllo dough, defrosted per package directions
½ cup finely chopped yellow onion
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F and place parchment on 2 baking sheets.
Heat olive oil in a skillet, add onion, and cook until soft.
Stir in garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in spinach until just wilted, then remove from heat and cool.
Add cheeses and seasonings to the cooled spinach mixture.
Arrange one sheet of phyllo dough on a clean work surface and brush with melted butter.
Cover the remaining phyllo dough with a damp towel.
Place a second sheet of phyllo on top and brush with butter, then place a third sheet on top.
Cut the buttered phyllo lengthwise into four strips.
Place about 1 tablespoon of the spinach mixture on the bottom of each strip.
Take the bottom right corner and fold the dough over the filling to make a triangle.
Fold the bottom left corner up to make another triangle.
Continue folding until all the dough is folded into a triangle shape.
Arrange the stuffed triangles seam-side down on the prepared baking sheet and lightly brush with butter.
Repeat with the remaining phyllo dough and spinach filling.
Bake the spanakopita triangles in the preheated oven until golden brown, about 20 minutes.
notes: Can be made ahead and refrigerated. Pop into oven 30 minutes before eating.
